surface.name: Chargebee Billing API Quickstart
surface.selection_basis: Chargebee's API Quickstart is the official developer route that installs one server-side SDK, creates a test customer and catalog, and ends with a subscription plus invoice. The page requires a peer language choice, so the common account spine is preserved and Node is reconstructed as one complete candidate.

alternatives_considered.name: Python API Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Python is a peer SDK tab and the guide does not rank it over Node or the other languages.

alternatives_considered.name: PHP, Go, Java, Ruby, and .NET API Quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are peer language implementations of the same customer, catalog, and subscription path.

alternatives_considered.name: AI-populated test-site onboarding
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This optional path derives demo catalog data from a public pricing page rather than following the API Quickstart's fixed sample resources.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Chargebee Billing test site and follow the API Quickstart until the selected SDK creates the sample subscription and associated paid invoice.
assumed_prior_state: No Chargebee account, test site, live site, site domain, administrator or owner role, API key, SDK installation, customer, test card, item family, item, item price, subscription, or invoice is assumed.
A valid business email, phone, company context, browser, and one supported SDK environment are available.
No signup form was submitted, terms were acknowledged, email was claimed, password or domain was set, API key was copied, SDK was installed, or API request was sent during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully create a subscription through the API Quickstart.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The test-site API response contains the created active subscription, its customer, the charged test card, and the generated paid invoice.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The final create-with-items response contains the sample subscription with `status` `active` and an associated invoice with `status` `paid`, after which the guide states that the subscription was successfully created.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Step 7: Create a subscription, followed by the created subscription, customer, charged card, and generated invoice response.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart explicitly ends after creating the sample subscription and returning its customer, card, and paid invoice. Later billing features and production setup are outside this first API route.

prerequisites.requirement: Provide name, valid business email, phone, monetization category, employee count, and a US, EU, or AU data-center selection on the free-trial form.
Claim the account from the Complete Account Setup email and set the password, unique site domain, and company name so the test site can be opened.
Use the Owner role automatically assigned to the site creator, or another owner or administrator role, to view and create API keys.
Choose and provide a supported Node, Python, PHP, Go, Java, Ruby, or .NET environment for the SDK-specific route.
Create a full-access API key in the Chargebee test site and keep it secret.

primary_path.action: Open the Chargebee API Quickstart and review its required account, test-site API key, SDK choice, and subscription terminal.
primary_path.output: The seven-step API route and peer SDK tabs are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide identifies a test site as the source of its first credential.

primary_path.action: Open the Chargebee trial signup page and enter the required business values and data-center choice.
details: The public form requests name, business email and phone, monetization category, employee count, and AU, US, or EU data center.
primary_path.success_signal: All required business, monetization, employee-count, and data-center inputs are populated.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public form does not enumerate field-level validation failures.

primary_path.action: Acknowledge the Terms of Service and Privacy Notice and select Complete Signup.
primary_path.input: Completed trial form and optional marketing choice
primary_path.success_signal: Chargebee begins the documented Complete Account Setup email flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No submission failure or retry behavior is documented for this form.

primary_path.action: Send the Complete Account Setup email to the registered business email address.
primary_path.success_signal: The Complete Account Setup message arrives at the registered address.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No delivery time or recovery instruction is documented on the selected sources.

primary_path.action: Open the email link, claim the account, and set the password, unique site domain, and company name.
details: The creator is automatically assigned the Owner role.
Completing the domain setup enables login to the test site.
primary_path.input: Complete Account Setup link and site configuration values
primary_path.output: Claimed Chargebee account and configured test site
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can log in to the Chargebee test site as its owner.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The site domain must be unique; the official sources do not enumerate a domain-collision recovery flow.

candidate_paths.name: Skip optional AI population and use the Node SDK
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This candidate resolves the optional pricing-page onboarding branch by choosing Skip and resolves the peer SDK choice by following the first displayed Node implementation.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Repeats the common trial, email-claim, and site-configuration spine, then continues through the documented Skip branch, full-access key creation, and Node Quickstart.

steps.action: Open the API Quickstart and its Chargebee free-trial link.
Enter name, valid business email, phone, applicable monetization category, employee count, and one of the US, EU, or AU data centers.
steps.failure_or_wait: The public form does not enumerate field-level validation failures.

steps.action: Choose the optional marketing setting, acknowledge the Terms of Service and Privacy Notice, and select Complete Signup.
steps.success_signal: The Complete Account Setup email flow begins.
steps.failure_or_wait: No submission failure or retry behavior is documented.

steps.action: Send the Complete Account Setup email.
steps.success_signal: The email arrives at the registered business address.

steps.action: Open the email link, claim the account, and set the password, unique site domain, and company name.
details: The site creator receives the Owner role automatically.
steps.success_signal: The owner can log in to the test site.
steps.failure_or_wait: The domain must be unique; no collision recovery is documented.

steps.action: Select Skip the setup when Chargebee offers pricing-page auto-population.
steps.input: Claimed account and optional onboarding decision
steps.output: Test-site dashboard without AI-generated catalog configuration
steps.success_signal: Chargebee takes the developer to the test-site dashboard.
steps.failure_or_wait: If no public pricing page is detected, Chargebee may prompt for a URL; the selected route uses the documented Skip action.

steps.action: Go to Settings, Configure Chargebee, API Keys and Events, API keys; select Add API Key, choose Full-Access Key, name it, and select Create Key.
details: Test and live API keys are distinct.
steps.input: Owner access to the test site
steps.success_signal: The new full-access key is listed for the test site.
steps.failure_or_wait: The key guide does not enumerate creation failures.

steps.action: Install the Node Chargebee SDK with the published npm, yarn, or pnpm command.
steps.success_signal: The package is available to the application.
steps.failure_or_wait: No installation recovery is documented in the Quickstart.

steps.action: Initialize the Chargebee client with the test-site name and full-access API key.
steps.success_signal: The client is configured for the test site.
steps.failure_or_wait: The guide's sample catches and prints request errors but does not add a separate configuration check.

steps.action: Run the published customer-create call for `acme-east` and `Acme Eastern`, enable auto-collection, and attach the published test card.
steps.input: Configured client and test card values
steps.failure_or_wait: The Node sample catches and prints customer-creation errors.

steps.action: Run the published item-family call to create `cloud-storage` named Cloud Storage.
steps.failure_or_wait: The sample catches the request error without a separate recovery flow.

steps.action: Run the published item call to create the `silver-plan` plan item inside the Cloud Storage family.
steps.failure_or_wait: The sample catches the request error without a separate recovery flow.

steps.action: Run the published item-price call to create `silver-plan-USD-monthly` at 50000 minor units every month.
steps.failure_or_wait: The sample catches the request error without a separate recovery flow.

steps.action: Run the published create-with-items call to subscribe `acme-east` to five units of `silver-plan-USD-monthly`.
steps.success_signal: The API returns subscription, customer, card, and invoice objects.
steps.failure_or_wait: The sample catches and prints subscription-creation errors.

steps.action: Verify that the returned subscription is `active`, the card was charged, and the generated invoice is `paid`.
steps.output: Confirmed sample subscription and paid invoice
steps.success_signal: The response matches the Quickstart's subscription, customer, card, and paid-invoice terminal.
steps.failure_or_wait: If the response is an error, use the JSON error details returned by Chargebee before retrying.

branches.path: Select AU, US, or EU on the signup form.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Determines the Chargebee account's data location before site creation.

branches.path: Review detected products, continue to pricing, continue to brand, proceed to sandbox, wait for sync, and review the generated catalog and brand.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds generated catalog data before the API Quickstart; the fixed sample resources still need distinct IDs.

branches.path: Select Skip the setup to go directly to the test-site dashboard without auto-configuration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: This is the shorter onboarding route reconstructed in the Node candidate.

branches.path: Follow the Python, PHP, Go, Java, Ruby, or .NET tab for the same customer, catalog, and subscription sequence.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation and code while preserving the API resources and terminal.

friction_gates.description: Trial signup requires business identity, monetization category, employee count, and data-center fields.
Submitting the trial acknowledges the Chargebee Terms of Service and Privacy Notice.
The signup form requires an AU, US, or EU data-center choice.
The site documentation requires claiming the account from the Complete Account Setup email.
A password and unique site domain must be set before test-site login.
Chargebee can offer Proceed or Skip for AI-populated pricing-page setup before the test-site dashboard.
Only a site administrator or owner can see the API keys needed by the Quickstart; the site creator is automatically the owner.
The Node route requires a test-site full-access key, which can create, retrieve, update, and delete API resources and must be kept secret.
The API Quickstart requires a Node, Python, PHP, Go, Java, Ruby, or .NET implementation choice.

sources.title: API Quickstart Guide
sections_used: Step 2: Download and set up the SDK
Step 3: Create a customer with a payment method

sources.title: Understanding Sites - Chargebee Docs
sections_used: Auto-Populate Test Site from your Pricing Page during Sign Up

sources.title: Sign Up - Get Your Free Sandbox - Chargebee
Users & Roles - Chargebee Docs
evidence_supported: Owner role is automatically assigned to the site creator

uncertainties.question: Which data center, optional pricing-page onboarding branch, and SDK should the developer choose?
uncertainties.impact: The data center changes data location, the Proceed branch adds generated catalog and branding actions, and the SDK changes installation and code.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Chargebee presents US, EU, and AU data centers, Proceed or Skip onboarding, and seven peer SDK tabs without selecting one universal combination; the record reconstructs Skip plus Node as one complete candidate.

excluded_after_success: Real gateway configuration, live-site enablement, production payment methods, taxes, hosted checkout, entitlements, dunning, webhooks, go-live review, and later subscription operations occur after the sample subscription and invoice.
